raw_consumer_statement:
I purchased a {$1800.00} LED medicine cabinet with XXXX from Wayfair using the Wayfair Credit Card ( issued by Citibank ) on XX/XX/XXXX. It was professionally installed on XX/XX/XXXX. By XXXX, the integrated lighting failed. A licensed electrician returned on XX/XX/XXXX, and diagnosed a sealed internal wiring defect. I reported the issue to Wayfair the same day, submitted photos, receipts, and a technician report, and followed up repeatedly. 

Wayfair failed to provide a replacement or refund despite multiple requests. I filed a formal dispute with Citibank on XX/XX/XXXX, and then resubmitted all documentation on XX/XX/XXXX, after being asked for more information. As of today ( XX/XX/XXXXXXXX  ), the issue remains unresolved. 

While waiting, I was forced to buy and install a temporary light fixture because the defective cabinet is still hardwired into the wall, and I must keep the switch off. Ive incurred expenses for installation, diagnosis, and additional lighting, and I am still paying for a product that does not function. 

Wayfair also suppressed my negative reviewdespite my attempt to post a 1-star rating, the lowest visible review remains 4 stars. This influenced my original decision to buy the item and raises concerns about deceptive review practices.

I am requesting a full charge reversal due to merchant non-performance.
